notes: Confirmed: the Blazor SKGLView is the only GL view implementation in SkiaSharp that does not expose GRContext as a public property. The field exists as 'private GRContext? context' on line 24 of SKGLView.razor.cs, but no public accessor is defined. All 15+ other GL view implementations across macOS, iOS, Android, WPF, WinForms, WinUI, Tizen, Uno, and MAUI expose 'public GRContext GRContext => context'. A workaround exists via e.Surface.Context pattern matching inside the paint callback, but this only works during OnPaintSurface and returns GRRecordingContext (base class), not GRContext.

reproductionSteps.description: Grep Blazor SKGLView.razor.cs for GRContext visibility — confirm field is private with no public accessor
Grep all platform GL views for public GRContext property — confirm every other platform exposes it publicly
Verify no public GRContext accessor exists anywhere in the Blazor view assembly
reproductionSteps.output: (no matches — confirms no public GRContext property exists in the Blazor views)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm workaround exists via SKSurface.Context which returns GRRecordingContext base class, accessible only inside paint callback

actionability.reason: Confirmed platform parity gap: Blazor is the only GL view without a public GRContext property. Fix is trivial (one-line property addition), but requires maintainer decision on whether to also expose glInterface and renderTarget.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and Blazor views labels

workarounds: Inside OnPaintSurface, use pattern matching: if (e.Surface.Context is GRContext grContext) { ... } — only works during the paint callback, not outside it.
